benchmark_protocol.source_generation: Each matrix generated one deterministic source workbook in a separate preparation process. Baseline and optimized source SHA-256 values matched for every profile in both orders.
benchmark_protocol.representative_policy: Measured run nearest total_editor_ms median; earliest run breaks ties.
benchmark_protocol.process_priority: Benchmark runner parents used Windows High priority and child benchmark processes inherited that priority.
benchmark_protocol.verification: Every matrix passed schema telemetry, output-plan byte accounting and raw compressed-payload checks. The representative output for every matrix opened with openpyxl after benchmark timing; formula profiles retained the recorded external hyperlink target.
benchmark_protocol.cache_measurement: Repeated runs were warmed, but no ETW trace or physical OS cache-hit attribution was collected. Only executable foreground read/wait telemetry is claimed.

validation.focused_tests: passed: package reader ZIP failures, public Patch cells and public save compression
validation.no_images_profile: build and image-disabled runtime smoke passed
validation.install_consumer_smoke: passed for production, stored-only and no-images installations

resource_lifecycle: No background worker was introduced. Pending OVERLAPPED I/O is cancelled and joined by local RAII state; benchmark sources, outputs, install consumers and detached baseline worktree were removed after evidence capture.
qualification: Elapsed time is system-sensitive. Claims are limited to the recorded same-machine warmed dual-order protocol and are not generalized to arbitrary workbooks, other machines, Office compatibility, physical cache-hit rate or full-feature superiority.
